title: 10 best herbal tinctures for oral thrust
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for oral thrush are concentrated liquid extracts that contain active compounds from herbs, which are used to treat and prevent oral thrush, a fungal infection in the mouth caused by Candida albicans. These tinctures offer a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments, which can have side effects. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with oral thrush include echinacea, goldenseal, and calendula. Echinacea boosts the immune system, helping the body fight off the infection, while goldenseal and calendula have antifungal properties that can inhibit the growth of Candida albicans. By using herbal tinctures, people can alleviate the symptoms of oral thrush, such as white patches on the tongue and inside of the cheeks, and improve their quality of life without relying on prescription medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with oral thrush because it has anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antiseptic properties that can help reduce the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Its astringent properties can also help to soothe inflamed and sore tissues in the mouth, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow tincture can help to boost the immune system, which can help the body to fight off the infection more effectively. Its natural properties make it a safer and gentler alternative to conventional antifungal medications, which can have side effects such as stomach upset and headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce the swelling and redness associated with oral thrush, providing relief from discomfort and pain. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial properties help inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush, by disrupting its cell membrane. The active constituents responsible for this property include alpha-bisabolol, chamazulene, and azulenes.

Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent properties help tighten and dry mucous membranes, reducing the moist environment that encourages the growth of Candida albicans. This is due to the tannins present in yarrow.

Immune-boosting: Yarrow tincture's immune-boosting properties help strengthen the body's natural defenses, allowing it to better fight off the Candida albicans infection. This is due to the presence of various polysaccharides, flavonoids, and alkaloids.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with oral thrush by numbing the affected area. This is due to the presence of lactones and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides.
Chop the yarrow into small pieces and place them in a clean, dry jar, filling it up to three-quarters capacity.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over the chopped yarrow, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle. Take 30 drops up to three times a day for oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in azulene, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for soothing oral thrush inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of yarrow contain flavonoids and tannins, which have astringent properties, aiding in reducing redness and irritation in oral thrush.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are a source of essential oils and alpha-pinene, providing antimicrobial effects, helpful in fighting the fungal infection causing oral thrush.
Roots: Yarrow roots have a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, contributing to the plant's antifungal properties, making them beneficial for treating oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with oral thrust because it creates a protective barrier in the mouth, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by the condition. The demulcent properties of marshmallow root extract help to soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes, promoting healing and providing relief from symptoms. Its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects also contribute to maintaining a healthy oral environment, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrust. Furthermore, marshmallow tincture can help reduce the frequency and severity of outbreaks, making it an effective natural remedy for managing oral thrust.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tincture forms a protective film over oral tissues, providing relief from pain and inflammation associated with oral thrush, due to its high mucilage content.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort in the mouth, thanks to the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the marshmallow plant.
Antimicrobial: Marshmallow tincture inhibits the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush, by disrupting the fungal cell membrane, owing to its tannins and polysaccharides.
Immune-boosting: The tincture enhances the immune system, helping the body fight off infections, as it contains various antioxidants and vitamins, such as vitamin C and A.
Soothing: Marshmallow tincture alleviates irritation and promotes healing in the mouth, due to its mucilaginous and emollient properties, which create a soothing effect on inflamed mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried marshmallow root into small pieces, then combine with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan; let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the plant material, then measure the remaining liquid and add equal parts of 80-proof alcohol (such as vodka), ensuring the final tincture contains at least 25% alcohol.
Mix the liquid well, then pour it into a sterilized glass jar or bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

After the recommended steeping time, strain the tincture again, discarding the plant material, and store the final product in a clean, sterilized glass container with a tight-fitting lid. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day or as needed for oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Marshmallow flowers contain mucilage, which can help soothe and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat.
Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant also contain mucilage, making them useful for creating a protective coating on mucous membranes and reducing irritation.
Roots: Marshmallow roots have a high mucilage content, which can help relieve pain and inflammation in the mouth and throat, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures for oral thrush.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds contain pectin, a type of fiber that can help soothe and heal irritated tissues in the mouth and throat.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains mucilage, which can help create a barrier between irritants and sensitive tissues in the mouth and throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tincture helps with oral thrush due to its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Oral thrush, a fungal infection caused by Candida albicans, can lead to the formation of white patches on the tongue, inner cheeks, and throat. The active compounds in wild indigo, such as alkaloids and isothiocyanates, work together to inhibit the growth of Candida, thus reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Furthermore, wild indigo's immune-boosting properties can help the body fight off infections more effectively, further aiding in the management of oral thrush.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Wild indigo tincture has the ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria, which is important for oral thrush as it helps to reduce the overgrowth of the Candida fungus that causes the condition. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the alkaloids present in the plant, such as indigo and indirubin.

Anti-inflammatory: Wild indigo tincture has been found to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the redness, swelling, and pain associated with oral thrush. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the isoflavonoids and other polyphenols present in the plant.

Immune-stimulating: Wild indigo tincture has been shown to stimulate the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections, including oral thrush. The immune-stimulating properties are attributed to the polysaccharides present in the plant.

Antifungal: Wild indigo tincture has antifungal properties, which can help to inhibit the growth of fungi, including the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush. The antifungal properties are attributed to the alkaloids and other compounds present in the plant.

Analgesic: Wild indigo tincture has been found to have analgesic properties, which can help to relieve the pain and discomfort associated with oral thrush. The analgesic properties are attributed to the alkaloids and other compounds present in the plant.
remedy_recipe: Harvest wild indigo plants, ensuring the above-ground portions are fully grown and have not yet flowered, typically in late spring or early summer.
Thoroughly rinse the harvested wild indigo plants to remove any dirt or debris, then roughly chop the plants into small pieces.
Fill a clean mason jar approximately 3/4 full with the chopped wild indigo plant material, then add 80-proof vodka until the jar is completely full, covering the plant material by at least 1 inch.
Seal the mason jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for a period of 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days to ensure even extraction.

After 4-6 weeks, strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the plant material and storing the resulting tincture in a clean, airtight glass bottle. Recommended dosage is 30-60 drops, 2-3 times daily, for oral thrush treatment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that have antimicrobial properties, making them effective for treating oral thrush.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe irritation and inflammation in the mouth.
Leaves: Have been used traditionally for their antifungal properties, making them useful for treating oral thrush caused by candida fungus.
Seeds: Contain essential oils with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage tincture helps with oral thrush because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal mouth irritations caused by the fungal infection. The tincture is derived from the leaves and flowers of the borage plant, which contains essential fatty acids, flavonoids, and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and fight off the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush. By using borage tincture as a mouthwash or applying it directly to the affected areas, it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, redness, and swelling, and promote faster healing. Additionally, borage tincture is a natural and safe alternative to conventional antifungal treatments, making it a popular choice for those seeking a more holistic approach to oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Borage tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its high gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) content, can help reduce inflammation associated with oral thrush.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of borage tincture, attributed to its tannins and phenolic acids, can help control the growth of Candida fungus causing oral thrush.
Immune-boosting: Borage tincture's ability to boost the immune system, thanks to its essential fatty acids and vitamins, can help the body fight off the oral thrush infection.

Demulcent: The demulcent properties of borage tincture, owing to its mucilage content, can help soothe and protect the irritated mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from oral thrush symptoms.

Wound-healing: Borage tincture's wound-healing properties, resulting from its rich supply of essential fatty acids and antioxidants, can aid in the healing process of any lesions caused by oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest borage flowers, ensuring they are fully open and free from pesticides, then rinse and dry them gently.
Fill a clean jar with dried borage flowers, leaving about an inch of space at the top, and add 80-proof vodka until the flowers are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers and transferring the liquid to a clean, dark glass bottle.

Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, the date it was made, and the recommended dosage (typically 20-30 drops, three times a day, for oral thrush), and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Borage flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for treating oral thrush.
Leaves: Borage leaves contain essential fatty acids and have antimicrobial properties, which help in managing oral thrush symptoms.
Seeds: Borage seeds are a great source of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has anti-inflammatory effects, aiding in the treatment of oral thrush.
Roots: Borage roots have mucilaginous properties, which can help soothe and protect the oral mucosa in cases of oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with oral thrush due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and wound-healing properties. The herb contains active compounds like flavonoids and essential oils, which inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, pot marigold tincture can alleviate symptoms such as pain, redness, and irritation in the mouth. Its soothing effects can help restore oral health and provide relief from the discomfort caused by oral thrush.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in the mouth, providing relief from the painful symptoms of oral thrush. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture inhibits the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The antimicrobial effect is attributed to the plant's flavonoids and essential oils.

Wound healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing in the mouth, helping to repair damaged tissue caused by oral thrush. This is due to the presence of the active constituent, triterpenoid, which has wound-healing properties.

Immune system support: Pot marigold tincture can help boost the immune system, which is essential for fighting off the Candida fungus. This is due to the presence of antioxidants in the plant, which support the immune system.

Pain relief: Pot marigold tincture can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with oral thrush. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils, which have analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, ensuring they are fully open and dry; rinse gently to remove dirt or insects.
Fill a clean jar with freshly harvested flowers, filling it three-quarters full, then pour 80-proof vodka over the flowers until they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days to ensure even extraction.
After the designated time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the spent flowers and transferring the liquid to a clean, amber glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the tincture's contents, date of preparation, and recommended dosage (typically 30-60 drops, three times daily, for oral thrush).
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for treating oral thrush infections.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have a soothing effect on inflamed oral tissue, providing relief from oral thrush symptoms.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils that have antifungal properties, helping to combat the Candida fungus responsible for oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with oral thrust because it is known to contain compounds that can help to strengthen the connective tissues in the mouth and throat. These tissues can become weakened in individuals with oral thrust, leading to the development of blisters and sores. By strengthening these tissues, gotu kola tincture can help to reduce the frequency and severity of oral thrust outbreaks. Additionally, gotu kola has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can further help to soothe and heal any existing sores or inflammation in the mouth. Overall, gotu kola tincture can be a helpful natural remedy for individuals seeking relief from the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrust.
remedy_properties: Wound healing promotion: Gotu kola tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for oral thrush as it can help soothe and heal inflamed oral tissues. The active constituents responsible for this property include asiaticoside, madecassoside, and centelloside.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Gotu kola tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate the redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with oral thrush. The triterpene acids, such as asiatic acid, madecassic acid, and maslinic acid, contribute to these anti-inflammatory effects.

Antimicrobial activity: Gotu kola tincture exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush. The flavonoids and tannins present in gotu kola, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been found to have antifungal properties.

Immunomodulatory effects: Gotu kola tincture can help regulate the immune response, which is crucial for managing oral thrush. The pentacyclic triterpene acids, including asiatic acid and madecassic acid, have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects.

Antioxidant properties: Gotu kola tincture contains antioxidants, which can help protect oral tissues from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation. The primary antioxidants in gotu kola are flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids, like caffeic and chlorogenic acids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried gotu kola leaves and add them to a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum over the chopped leaves until they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent leaves.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Take 30-60 drops, 1-3 times daily, for oral use.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Gotu kola leaves are the most commonly used part of the plant in making medicinal tinctures for oral thrush due to their high triterpene content, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of gotu kola contain asiaticoside, which has been shown to have wound healing properties, making it useful for treating oral thrush.

Rhizomes: Gotu kola rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine to treat various skin conditions, including those caused by fungal infections like oral thrush, due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with oral thrush due to its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal remedy contains active compounds, such as echinacea, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. By reducing inflammation and combating the overgrowth of this fungus, the tincture helps alleviate symptoms of oral thrush, such as white patches, soreness, and irritation in the mouth. The herbal extract's soothing and healing effects promote a faster recovery and maintain overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Eastern purple coneflower tincture contains active constituents such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which exhibit antimicrobial effects, helping to reduce the presence of harmful bacteria associated with oral thrust.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of compounds like cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid in the tincture provides anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate inflammation and swelling in the oral cavity caused by oral thrust.

Immunostimulant properties: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help boost the immune system, primarily due to the polysaccharides and essential oils it contains, enhancing the body's ability to fight off the Candida fungus responsible for oral thrust.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect oral tissues from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall oral health and aiding in the management of oral thrust.

Wound healing properties: The tincture's ability to promote wound healing, attributed to its tannins and essential oils, can help soothe and heal irritated oral tissues, providing relief from oral thrust symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully open and at peak bloom, typically in late summer.
Rinse harvested plant material, then chop into small pieces and fill a clean, dry glass jar, leaving 1-2 inches of space at the top.
Prepare a 50/50 solution of pure alcohol (such as vodka or grain alcohol) and filtered water, then pour the solution over the chopped coneflower, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, occasionally shaking the jar to help the extraction process.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a clean, amber glass dropper bottle. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture orally, as needed, for oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in beneficial compounds, the flowers of the eastern purple coneflower are used to make medicinal tinctures for oral thrush due to their potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: High in therapeutic constituents, the leaves of the eastern purple coneflower are used in the preparation of medicinal tinctures for oral thrush, owing to their soothing and healing effects on inflamed mucous membranes.

Roots: Packed with active compounds, the roots of the eastern purple coneflower are used in making medicinal tinctures for oral thrush, thanks to their powerful antifungal and immune-boosting properties.

Seeds: Containing valuable medicinal components, the seeds of the eastern purple coneflower are used to create medicinal tinctures for oral thrush, as they help alleviate infection and promote tissue regeneration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tincture helps with oral thrust because it has been traditionally used to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. Its active compounds, eleutherosides, are believed to stimulate the production of immune cells and enhance their activity, which can help the body fight off infections and reduce inflammation in the mouth. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has been shown to have adaptogenic properties, meaning it can help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. By boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation, Siberian ginseng tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of oral thrust and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Siberian ginseng tincture helps the body adapt to stress and boosts the immune system, which is important for oral thrush as it can be caused or worsened by a weakened immune system. The active constituents responsible for this property are eleutherosides and polysaccharides.

Antimicrobial property: Siberian ginseng has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help control the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush. The antimicrobial properties come from the presence of saponins and phenolic compounds.

Anti-inflammatory property: Siberian ginseng tincture can reduce inflammation, which is beneficial for oral thrush as the condition can cause painful inflammation in the mouth. The anti-inflammatory effects are attributed to eleutherosides and ginsenosides.

Immunomodulatory property: Siberian ginseng can help regulate and balance the immune system, which is crucial for managing oral thrush. The immunomodulatory properties are due to eleutherosides, polysaccharides, and ginsenosides.

Wound healing property: Siberian ginseng tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for oral thrush as it can cause painful lesions and sores in the mouth. The wound healing property is attributed to the presence of ginsenosides and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Siberian ginseng root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the ginseng, ensuring the herb is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and use 1-2 mL up to three times daily for oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of eleutherosides, which are believed to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making them effective for treating oral thrush.
Rhizomes: Rich in saponins, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat the Candida fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Seeds: Contain various bioactive compounds, including phenylpropanoids and lignans, which have been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, useful for managing oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with oral thrust because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When used as a tincture, licorice can help to reduce the inflammation and discomfort associated with oral thrust. Additionally, licorice has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the presence of harmful bacteria in the mouth. This can help to prevent the recurrence of oral thrust and promote overall oral health. The soothing and healing properties of licorice tincture make it a valuable tool in managing oral thrust and promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice tincture forms a protective film over the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by oral thrush. This property is due to the high mucilage content in licorice.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tincture inhibits the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush, by disrupting its cell membrane. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture reduces inflammation and swelling in the mouth, providing relief from the discomfort of oral thrush. This is attributed to the presence of glycyrrhizin, which inhibits the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Expectorant: Licorice tincture helps remove excess mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms associated with oral thrush. This property is due to the glycyrrhizic acid content in licorice.

Immunomodulatory: Licorice tincture supports the immune system, helping the body fight off infections, including oral thrush. The active constituent responsible for this property is glycyrrhizin, which enhances the activity of immune cells.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the licorice root until fully submerged.
Cover the jar with a tight-fitting lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean glass bottle, discarding the spent herbs. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it effective for treating oral thrush.
Seeds: Licorice seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their demulcent and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of oral thrush.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating oral infections like thrush.
Leaves: Licorice leaves contain flavonoids and coumarins, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tincture helps with oral thrush due to its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains saponins and polycyclic polyphenols, which have been found to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. By reducing inflammation and combating the overgrowth of this fungus, English ivy tincture can help alleviate the discomfort and symptoms associated with oral thrush, such as white patches, redness, and pain in the mouth or throat. Its soothing and healing effects can promote a faster recovery and restore oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: English ivy tincture has the ability to kill or inhibit the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria and viruses, which can help reduce the presence of harmful microbes in the mouth that cause oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins, terpenoids, and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the mouth associated with oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids.

Immune-boosting: English ivy tincture can help strengthen the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections such as oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyphenols, flavonoids, and triterpenes.

Antifungal: English ivy tincture has antifungal properties that can help inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins, terpenoids, and flavonoids.

Wound-healing: The tincture has wound-healing properties that can help promote the healing of sores and lesions in the mouth caused by oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, saponins, and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest English ivy leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides; rinse and dry them.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried ivy leaves into small pieces, then place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the ground ivy leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged; seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks.
Shake the jar daily to help the extraction process; after the designated time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin into a clean, dry glass bottle or jar.
Store the final ivy tincture in a cool, dark place; use 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) orally, up to three times a day, for oral thrush treatment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy plant contain saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help treat oral thrush.
Stems: The stems of the English ivy plant contain tannins and saponins, which have astringent and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of oral thrush.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy plant contain phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help treat oral thrush.

Bark: The bark of the English ivy plant contains alkaloids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help soothe irritation and inflammation in the mouth caused by oral thrush.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oral thrush with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of antifungal and immune-boosting herbs. A popular choice is a tincture made from extracts of goldenseal, echinacea, and calendula. Goldenseal has strong antifungal properties that can help eliminate the Candida fungus causing thrush. Echinacea can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the infection. Calendula has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal irritated oral tissues. Always consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oral thrush that are treated with herbal tinctures include candidiasis and other fungal infections. Herbs such as oregano, goldenseal, and echinacea have antifungal and immune-boosting properties that can help alleviate symptoms. For instance, oregano oil has been found to have strong antifungal effects against Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Similarly, goldenseal and echinacea can help strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment.
